mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points in a single month signals acute labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.4pp, or the signal fails to separate winners from losers (Sharpe < 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which drives corporate earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over rolling 3-year windows, or positive returns do not materialize when both series expand concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side bottlenecks and reduced capacity utilization, forcing equity valuations lower over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: INDPRO contractions during tightening periods produce positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of cases, or the signal's predictive power vanishes outside the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the consumer price index exceeding 0.2% signals rapid disinflation, prompting immediate equity portfolio rebalancing toward risk assets and positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to generate a positive information coefficient over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month declines in total nonfarm payrolls indicate weakening labor demand and reduced household income growth, which systematically depresses corporate revenue expectations and S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS declines correlate with positive 60-day forward returns in > 55% of observations, or the rank correlation remains statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms broad-based economic expansion, reinforcing earnings momentum and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns in > 60% of triggered cases, or the interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample predictive accuracy over single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in industrial production during a high-inflation regime signal stagflationary pressures, forcing investors to discount future cash flows aggressively and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines in high-inflation periods yield positive 5-day returns in > 50% of cases, or the signal's predictive power disappears when inflation exceeds 4.0%.
